title: Missile Attack in Israel Hurts People Near Nuclear Center
summary: Iran fired missiles at two towns in Israel near a big nuclear research center. This happened late on a Saturday. At least seven people were badly hurt, and many buildings were damaged. Israel's leader said it was a hard evening and sent more help. This is the first time the nuclear center has been hit in the fighting. The war started a few weeks ago and is not close to ending. Iran also hit a faraway military base, showing its missiles can go very far. The war is making food and fuel prices go up around the world. Rescue workers helped people from damaged buildings, and many went to hospitals.

questions.correct_answer: Food and fuel prices are going up

background_read: This article is about a missile attack by Iran on Israel. Iran and Israel are countries in the Middle East that have been fighting in a war. A nuclear research center is a place where scientists work on nuclear energy, which can be used for power or weapons. Missiles are long-range weapons that can cause a lot of damage. When countries fight, it can hurt people and damage buildings, and it can also affect prices around the world. Rescue workers are heroes who help save people during emergencies like attacks.
Article_Structure: WHO: Iran attacked Israel, and people like Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and rescue workers were involved.
WHAT: Missiles hit towns near a nuclear research center, hurting people and damaging buildings.
WHERE: The attack happened in Dimona and Arad in Israel, and Iran also hit a military base in the Indian Ocean.
WHY: The war between Iran and Israel is ongoing, and this attack shows the conflict is getting more dangerous.

perspectives.description: Israel sees the attack as a serious threat, with leaders calling it difficult and sending emergency help.
Iran is showing it can strike far targets, possibly to demonstrate its military power in the war.
